name: Embalse de Alloz – return from Puente la Reina
name_original: Embalse de Alloz – vuelta desde Puente la Reina
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Puente La Reina/Gares offer a diverse topography, including rolling hills, farmland, wooded areas, vineyards, and olive groves typical of the Navarre region. The town is bisected by the Arga River, which features prominently in many scenic routes. Cyclists can also encounter the challenging Alto del Perdón mountain pass, known for its views. This variety provides constantly changing scenery for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Puente La Reina/Gares The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de Alloz – return from Puente la Reina, a 22.2 miles (35.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views around the Alloz reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge – Estella-Lizarra loop from Puente la Reina / Gares, a moderate 26.3 miles (42.4 km) path. This route passes by the historic Romanesque Bridge and through charming villages with historical architecture. Local road cyclists also love the Royal Palace of Olite – Puente la Reina loop from Puente la Reina / Gares, a 56.6 miles (91.1 km) trail leading through vineyards and historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 9 minutes. Road cycling around Puente La Reina/Gares is defined by rolling hills, vineyards, the Arga River, and the cultural heritage of the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance rides. The routes in Puente La Reina/Gares are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 15 reviews. More than 2,400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puente La Reina/Gares's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: The region offers a diverse topography for road cyclists, including rolling hills, farmland, wooded areas, vineyards, and olive groves. The Arga River bisects the town, and routes often incorporate scenic river paths. You can also encounter the challenging Alto del Perdón mountain pass, known for its rewarding views.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Puente La Reina/Gares, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from moderate loops to more challenging long-distance rides.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: Yes, Puente La Reina/Gares offers several easy road cycling routes. For instance, there are 4 routes specifically categorized as easy, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer cultural and historical immersion. You can pass by the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/119191" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilgrim Monument at Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/399423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Saint John the Baptist, Obanos&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1019580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge and Road of Andelos&lt;/a&gt; also offer a glimpse into the region's ancient history.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be warm, while winters are cooler but still offer opportunities for cycling, especially on lower elevation routes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes starting from Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in Puente La Reina/Gares. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24676037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Reina Romanesque Bridge – Estella-Lizarra loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through charming villages and past historical architecture.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in this area?
A: The routes around Puente La Reina/Gares cater to various abilities. You'll find 35 moderate routes, offering a good balance of challenge and enjoyment, and 20 difficult routes for experienced cyclists seeking significant elevation gains and longer distances.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 15 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse topography, the blend of natural beauty with cultural heritage, and the well-maintained routes that offer constantly changing scenery.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road cycling in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: While some routes can be challenging, the region does offer easier loops and sections suitable for families. The 'greenways' (Vías Verdes), though not exclusively road cycling, provide well-paved surfaces and gentle gradients, which can be excellent for family outings.

Q: Where can I find scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: The region is rich with scenic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3690724" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alto del Perdón&lt;/a&gt; is particularly renowned for its spectacular panoramic views. Many routes also traverse rolling hills and open farmland, offering picturesque landscapes throughout your ride.

Q: Is it possible to rent a road bike in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: Yes, Puente La Reina/Gares is a popular stop on the Camino de Santiago, and as such, there are usually options for bike rental services available in town to cater to pilgrims and tourists alike.

Q: Are there cafes or restaurants suitable for cyclists in Puente La Reina/Gares?
A: Puente La Reina/Gares, being a significant point on the Camino de Santiago, has a good selection of cafes, restaurants, and bars that welcome cyclists. You'll find places to refuel and relax after your ride, often with outdoor seating.

Q: What are the public transport options for reaching Puente La Reina/Gares with a bike?
A: Public transport options, such as regional buses, often connect Puente La Reina/Gares with larger cities in Navarre. It's advisable to check with the specific bus operators regarding their policies for transporting bicycles, as space can be limited or require prior booking.
tips:
Three generations of roads can be found here: the old Roman road and Roman bridge, then the provincial road NA-1110 and the A-12 motorway.
How many people have crossed this bridge for about 2,000 years?!
Precious panoramic views from the road towards the reservoir and against the Alloz reservoir and the valley of the Salado river.
Beautiful panoramic views of the valley from the 750 masl that are reached when you reach Alto San Cristóbal. From here descending to Lerate you will have wonderful views of the reservoir.
